data.description:
## Problem
Running `rebar metrics` with NO `--since/--until` (the default invocation) silently breaks every ticket-derived (agent_process) metric. The CLI defaults the flags to empty strings and passes them into the readers; `metrics.event_metrics._bounds("")` calls `datetime.fromisoformat("")` which raises `ValueError: Invalid isoformat string: ''`. The CLI's per-metric fault isolation catches it and renders each metric as `unavailable` with reason="Invalid isoformat string: ''", so the failure is silent.

## Reproduced (in the rebar repo itself)
`rebar metrics --output json` -> attempts_per_ticket / first_pass_rate / revert_recovery / reopen_recovery / rework_within_7_days all report `{unavailable: {reason: "Invalid isoformat string: ''"}}`.
With explicit dates (`--since 2026-01-01 --until 2026-12-31`) they compute correctly (e.g. first_pass_rate -> 0.9984). The git readers guard the empty string (churn returns data); the event/sidecar readers do not — an inconsistency.

## Expected
With no `--since/--until`, default the window to the **last 30 days** (until=today, since=today-30d) rather than passing empty strings. Empty/whitespace date values should normalize to this default (or to `None` open bounds) at the CLI boundary so no reader ever receives an unparseable string.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `rebar metrics` with no date flags returns real values for the agent_process metrics over a last-30-days window (no 'Invalid isoformat' unavailable).
- [ ] Default window documented in `rebar metrics --help`, user-guide, and the help text file.
- [ ] Explicit `--since/--until` still override the default; explicit empty-string values are handled gracefully.
- [ ] Unit/interface test covering the no-flags invocation (regression: currently masked as unavailable).
data.title: rebar metrics with no --since/--until fails ticket-derived metrics; default to last 30 days
